preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

sections.name: Global Navigation
sections.rationale: Opens every page. For Validator archetype at the decision stage, the NavigationHeader should use a persistent CTA (e.g., 'Start Free Trial') that remains visible as the founder scrolls through proof and pricing. This anchors conversion intent throughout the session.

sections.name: Hero with Trust Signal
sections.rationale: Establishes context above the fold for a Validator at the decision stage. The Validator variant of HeroPrimary always includes a trust_signal — critical for early-stage founders who need immediate reassurance before committing to a paid plan. Headline should frame the decisive value proposition, not introduce the product. The with-social-proof variant places a LogoStrip beneath the CTA to compound credibility at first glance.

sections.name: Credibility Proof Bar
sections.rationale: Directly beneath the hero, the SocialProofBar serves the Validator archetype at the decision stage by anchoring external credibility early. The Validator variant leads with a featured quote from a recognizable founder or company, with logos below — this layered proof addresses the skepticism of a founder about to commit budget. Placement here prevents trust decay before the argument deepens.

sections.name: Outcome Metrics
sections.rationale: Validators at the decision stage need quantified evidence, not promises. The StatBlock with source attribution (mandatory for Validator contexts) translates product value into concrete, verifiable outcomes — e.g., '38% reduction in churn, Source: internal cohort study Q1 2024'. For early-stage founders weighing ROI against runway, sourced stats convert abstract value into a defensible business case. 3-stat layout keeps it scannable.

sections.name: Capability Proof Grid
sections.rationale: At the decision stage, a Validator founder is comparing feature sets across shortlisted tools. The FeatureGrid with outcome-oriented benefit statements (not feature labels) maps capabilities directly to the founder's operational pain points. This section answers 'does it do what I need?' with structured, scannable specificity — the final capability check before pricing review.

sections.name: Decision-Point Testimonial
sections.rationale: A single, high-fidelity testimonial placed immediately before pricing serves the Validator archetype at the decision stage as a peer-validation trigger. TestimonialSingle with photo, role, and company logo gives the early-stage founder a relatable proof point — ideally from a peer founder who converted and saw results. Positioned at this decision moment, it directly reduces purchase anxiety.

sections.name: Pricing
sections.rationale: The core decision-stage conversion element for Validators. PricingCard in Validator variant includes plan descriptions and detailed feature lists — early-stage founders need to understand exactly what they're paying for before committing limited capital. The highlighted variant marks the recommended plan, and with-comparison supports tier differentiation. Transparency here is the ultimate Validator trust signal.

sections.name: Final Conversion Banner & Footer
sections.rationale: CTABanner in Validator variant closes the page with a subhead reiterating the core value proposition and a trust_signal such as 'No credit card required' or '14-day money-back guarantee' — removing final friction for a decision-stage founder. FooterMinimal with the with-cta variant recaptures any departing user who scrolled past pricing without converting. Together they form a two-layer safety net at the page terminal.

page.reasoning: Decision stage requires conversion focus. Notes state 'minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only' and 'The persistent CTA in nav is the most converted element on most SaaS pages.'
Validator archetype requires trust signals. Notes specify 'Validator variant always includes trust_signal' and 'with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster.'

slots.headline: Everything you need. Nothing you'll pay twice for.
slots.subhead: Six capabilities that remove the bottlenecks early-stage teams hit most before product-market fit.

features.title: Intent-Aware Components
features.description: Components that adapt to user context automatically — so your UI makes the right call without custom logic every time.

features.title: Ship-Ready Tokens
features.description: Design tokens pre-mapped to code variables, cutting design-to-dev sync from days to a single handoff file.

features.title: Usage Analytics Built In
features.description: See which components your team ships most, so you invest in patterns that actually move the product forward.

features.title: Role-Based Access Control
features.description: Grant designers, engineers, and stakeholders exactly the access they need — no over-sharing, no version chaos.

features.title: Continuous Sync with Figma
features.description: Two-way sync keeps your design file and codebase in agreement — no more 'which version is live?' conversations.

features.title: Audit-Ready Documentation
features.description: Auto-generated component docs that satisfy enterprise procurement checklists and accelerate your first big customer close.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype requires attributed credibility. Notes state 'Always include role and company for Validator credibility — anonymous quotes carry no weight' and 'with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity.'

slots.quote: We were 8 weeks from launch with three engineers and zero design system. MODE got us from scattered Figma frames to a coherent, documented component library in under two weeks. Our enterprise pilot didn't flag a single UI inconsistency during security review.

page.reasoning: Decision stage requires clear recommendation path. Notes specify 'highlighted variant for recommended plan' and 'Validator variant includes description and longer feature list' for informed choice.

slots.description: Built for early-stage SaaS teams that need a production-grade design system without a full-time design-ops hire. Everything to ship consistently and close your first 50 customers.

page.reasoning: Validator at decision point needs reassurance. Notes state 'Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required')' to reduce conversion friction.
